name: web3.py
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); requires RPC endpoint (Infura, Alchemy, or self-hosted node)

auth.notes: API key for RPC provider (Infura/Alchemy); private key for transaction signing

performance.notes: RPC call latency depends on provider; Ethereum mainnet state reads ~100ms

agent_readiness.notes: Official Python library for Ethereum and EVM-compatible blockchain interaction. Read contract state, send transactions, decode events, sign messages, interact with ENS. ABI encoding/decoding. Async support. EIP-1559 gas estimation. Works with any EVM chain (Ethereum, Polygon, Base, Arbitrum). Used in DeFi bots, analytics, smart contract testing, and blockchain data ingestion.

gotchas: Private key management is developer's responsibility — no key custody
Gas estimation can underestimate for complex contracts
EVM chain differences (reorg depths, finality) require chain-specific handling
RPC rate limits from providers can throttle data pipelines
best_when: DeFi protocol automation (swaps, liquidity management, yield farming)
Blockchain data ingestion for analytics pipelines
Smart contract interaction in Python-based agents
avoid_when: Non-EVM chains (use chain-specific SDKs for Solana, Sui, etc.)
TypeScript/JS projects (use ethers.js or viem instead)
summary: web3.py is the official Python library for Ethereum and EVM blockchain interaction. Read contract state, send transactions, decode events, and interact with DeFi protocols. Async support, EIP-1559 gas estimation, ABI encoding. Works with any EVM chain via RPC providers (Infura, Alchemy). Used in DeFi automation, blockchain analytics, and smart contract testing.
